title: Indianapolis

jokes.text: How about just you? Thank you, Ron. Yes.
jokes.setup: Leslie over-enthusiastically plans multiple speaking order permutations for the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt dismissal cuts through her overplanning with minimal words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - cuts through Leslie's verbosity with deadpan efficiency

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We could do it alphabetically by first name, or by last name, or by department, or by tenure, or we could do a rotating system where each person speaks for exactly four minutes and thirty seconds...

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: I couldn't care less about the commendation. But Indianapolis is home to Charles Mulligan's steakhouse
jokes.setup: Audience expects Ron to explain why he doesn't care about the award
jokes.punchline: His only motivation is steak, immediately pivoting from civic duty to carnivorous obsession
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron Swanson - perfect character integration where his meat obsession trumps everything else

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't care about awards. My only motivation is steak. Everything else is just noise.

jokes.text: I have taken a picture of every steak I've ever eaten there
jokes.punchline: The absurd level of documentation for steak consumption
jokes.explanation: Absurdist commitment to steak documentation feels perfectly in character for Ron

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I have a document here that details every steak I've eaten in the last 20 years. Cut, weight, temperature, sauce selection, establishment, date, time of day, and my emotional state before consumption.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I'm currently on my 847th steak. I have a five-year projection that accounts for seasonal availability and my declining digestive capabilities.

jokes.text: The lady next to me: A bitch. Specifically, my ex-wife Tammy.
jokes.setup: Ron cataloguing details of his steak photos
jokes.punchline: Casual escalation from 'bitch' to revealing it's his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with escalating reveal - economical and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I've been cataloguing these steak photos by marbling quality, cooking temperature, and the bitch who took half my stuff in the divorce.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tammy Two.

jokes.text: Oh, am I wearing an ascot? I didn't notice.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Tom's fancy appearance
jokes.punchline: Fake casual dismissal of his obviously deliberate fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom vanity masked as casual indifference

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: What? This old thing? I just threw it on.

jokes.text: His ether-based perfume 'Blackout' was named one Maxim's top 100 ways to trick someone into sex
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of a deeply problematic 'achievement'
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy disguised as innocent exposition - the casual delivery makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Dennis Feinstein has accomplished so much. He's built an empire, created thousands of jobs, and revolutionized the candy industry.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Plus, he's only been indicted three times.

jokes.text: 'Hey, Doug from Bloomington is thinking about buying a shirt.' I mean, come on, Doug, who cares?
jokes.punchline: Specific mundane Facebook update that perfectly captures meaningless social media
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific example that nails the absurdity of social media oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know what I hate about Facebook? It's all these pointless status updates from people you haven't talked to in ten years. Like, 'Just made a sandwich.' Great. Thanks for sharing that with 847 of your closest friends.

jokes.text: take him under your tiny little wing
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Tom to help Ben socially
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Tom's small stature
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy callback to Tom's height

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, I need you to help Ben be more social. He's too stiff, too formal.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Leslie, I'd love to help, but I'm only 5'4". How am I supposed to reach him?

jokes.text: April Ludgate Is the Best Ever, Volume One
jokes.setup: Someone asking about Andy's band's new album
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and over-the-top romantic album title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy romantic gesture - absurd but sweet

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, our new album is called 'Love's Tender Embrace: A Journey Through the Moonlit Gardens of My Heart Where Every Flower Represents a Different Aspect of Your Beauty and Also There's a Song About Your Eyes, Specifically the Left One'

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's a double album.

jokes.text: I kind of forgot that you need money when you have a girlfriend
jokes.setup: Andy realizing the financial implications of dating
jokes.punchline: Innocent admission of not understanding basic relationship economics
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent cluelessness about adult responsibilities

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, so when you're dating someone, you have to, like, pay for things? For both of you?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I thought that was just a thing rich people did.

jokes.text: different kinds of lubes that warm up when you rub 'em on stuff
jokes.setup: Andy listing gifts he wants to give April
jokes.punchline: Innocent but inappropriate mention of intimate products
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent delivery of inappropriate content

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I want to get April a gift that says 'I love you' but also 'I respect your personal space.' So I'm thinking a high-end vibrator.

jokes.text: I don't know. I guess we're dating. It's new. Whatever. I don't like labels.
jokes.setup: April being asked about her relationship status
jokes.punchline: Typical April disaffected response to emotional topics
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - deadpan dismissal of romantic feelings

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm in a relationship. It's fine. He's fine. Everything's fine. I don't really like talking about my feelings because then I have to have feelings about my feelings, and I don't have time for that.

jokes.text: In high school, they used to call me Angela Lansbury. But that was because of my haircut.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the nickname was about appearance, not deduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with specific reference that works on multiple levels

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I've always had a knack for detective work. In high school, they called me 'Sherlock'.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Because of your deductive skills?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: No, because I had really big teeth and a long face.

jokes.text: The owner of the motel I'm staying at said she was gonna screen Hope Floats in the lobby. Asked me if I wanted to watch.
jokes.punchline: Pathetically sad motel entertainment option
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail for Ben's sad temporary living situation

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm not going to some pathetically sad motel. I'm going to the Motel 6. They leave the light on for you.

jokes.text: I think she's gonna murder you.
jokes.setup: Ben describing the motel owner's invitation
jokes.punchline: Tom's matter-of-fact assessment of danger
jokes.explanation: Casual delivery of dark possibility

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: A motel owner inviting us to his room? That's not a Red Flag, that's a Red Flag factory. That's where Red Flags are manufactured.

jokes.text: Dame Gervin's Misshapen Celebrity Castle. It's where Madame Tussaud sends all of its failed wax figures
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific concept of failed wax figure disposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept that feels uniquely Parks and Rec

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: So we're driving past this place called 'The Wax Museum of Broken Dreams,' and I'm like, 'Finally, a venue that understands my aesthetic.' Turns out it's just where they throw away the failed wax figures. Which, honestly, is even better. Do you know how they dispose of them? They have this whole system. They melt them down, but apparently some of the wax is mixed with human hair and teeth from the 1970s, so they can't just dump it anywhere. There's actually a specific landfill in Nevada that's designated exclusively for failed celebrity wax figure disposal. It's called the 'Hall of Shame Valley.' I want to be buried there.

jokes.text: there are flecks of meat in my mustache, and I refuse to clean it because every now and then, a piece of meat will fall into my mouth
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his devotion to Mulligan's steakhouse
jokes.punchline: Disgustingly specific detail about mustache meat storage
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson meat obsession with genuinely gross specificity

jokes.text: Ron doesn't even have a front door. He won't even tell me his address.
jokes.punchline: Ron's extreme privacy extending to basic home features
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Ron's anti-government paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, you need a doorbell. What if someone important needs to reach you?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: If they're important, they'll know not to visit. I have a moat.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You don't have a moat.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Not yet.

jokes.text: There's a lot of men's coats in here. I like coats.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovering suspicious evidence in Chris's closet
jokes.punchline: Chris's weak deflection about coat preferences
jokes.explanation: Awkward deflection that builds tension

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Chris, why do you have seventeen identical cardigans in your closet?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Well, Leslie, I've found that a quality cardigan is a wardrobe staple. Each one is precisely the same thread count and—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: They still have the tags on them.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I'm very careful with my clothing investments.

jokes.text: Tom Hammen... Hammenstein. Haverford.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to name-drop Tom for free drinks
jokes.punchline: Fumbling Tom's already fake-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Ben's social awkwardness compounded by Tom's pretentious name

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know Tom Haverford. Tell him Ben Wyatt sent you and you'll get a discount.

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: Tom Haverford? Never heard of him.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What? No, Tom... Haverford. He's very well-known.

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: That's the fakest name I've ever heard.

jokes.text: They just boarded her up like she was some common warehouse.
jokes.setup: Ron discovering his beloved steakhouse is closed
jokes.punchline: Treating the restaurant closure like a personal tragedy
jokes.explanation: Ron's emotional investment in meat establishments

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: My whole life has been a lie. Everything I believed in, everything I stood for... gone. Closed. Just like that.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't know who I am anymore. That steakhouse was the only place that understood me. The only place that got it.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: They took away my sanctuary. My temple. My reason for living between the hours of six and nine PM.

jokes.text: What happened to the steaks that were in there when they closed? Do you think they got eaten?
jokes.punchline: Genuine concern for the welfare of inanimate steaks
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist Ron - anthropomorphizing meat products

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Genuine concern for the welfare of inanimate steaks

jokes.text: his real name is Dante Fiero, but he changed it to Dennis Feinstein 'cause that's way more exotic in Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Reverse cultural assumptions about what sounds exotic
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on cultural perceptions and small-town dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what's crazy? All these fancy French perfume names. 'Eau de Toilette.' 'Chanel No. 5.' Meanwhile, the most exotic thing I could name a perfume is 'Essence of Eagleton.'

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: That's not exotic, that's just the next town over.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Exactly! To someone in France, it IS exotic. 'Ooh, what is this mysterious scent?' 'Oh, it's just called Eagleton, darling.'

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Looks like you just nailed your future.
jokes.setup: Tom's plan to pitch his cologne
jokes.punchline: Awkward pickup-line approach to business networking
jokes.explanation: Tom's misguided confidence in terrible pickup lines

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm calling it 'Cologne Number Five.' It's got notes of ambition, a hint of desperation, and that fresh scent of a guy who's about to pitch you something whether you want to hear it or not.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Tom, that's not how you sell cologne.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh really, Jerry? Because I just sprayed some on you and you're still talking to me, which means it's working.

jokes.text: I'm a girl in a sleazy club.
jokes.setup: Andy asking how April can get free drinks
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of bar dynamics
jokes.explanation: April's blunt assessment of social reality

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: How do you always get free drinks?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I have a system. I go to a bar, I find the saddest guy there, and I tell him he looks like my dead dad. Then I cry a little, and he buys me drinks all night.

jokes.text: My name? Oprah. I said I want to drink alone.
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake name followed by blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - absurd lie with zero interest in politeness

dialogue.speaker: Creepy Guy
dialogue.line: Hi there, I'm Dirk Thunderbottom.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: That's the worst name I've ever heard. Go away.

jokes.text: I told him I had a pork deficiency.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining how he got food from a waiter
jokes.punchline: Made-up medical condition for free appetizers
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly stupid Andy logic for scamming free food

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So I told the waiter I had a very rare condition where I could only eat appetizers. He felt so bad for me, he kept bringing them out for free all night.

jokes.text: Since I am not a rabbit, no, I do not.
jokes.punchline: Logical rejection based on species classification
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron anti-vegetable stance with perfect logic

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ron, try some of this salad. It's got kale, quinoa, and a light vinaigrette.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Chris, I respect you, but I'm going to have to stop you right there. A salad is what food eats. I don't eat salad. I eat red meat and precious metals.

jokes.text: Well, Yoda wouldn't actually need networking. I mean, his powers were more spiritual. Shut up, you nerd!
jokes.punchline: Ben's nerdy correction followed by Tom's embarrassed reaction
jokes.explanation: Character dynamics - Ben's nerdiness vs Tom's false confidence

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm like Yoda, man. I'm wise beyond my years.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yoda is 900 years old. You're 37.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Well, you know what I mean.

jokes.text: You know, legally, if you're from the FDA, you have to tell me.
jokes.punchline: Confused legal understanding about undercover operations
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's paranoia and legal confusion

dialogue.speaker: Dennis Feinstein
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'undercover'? I thought that's when cops dress up like regular people to catch criminals.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: That's... yes, that's what undercover means.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis Feinstein
dialogue.line: Then how am I supposed to do it with my cologne? Do I dress the cologne up like a regular person?

jokes.text: That guy just pitched me a cologne called 'Sluts.' I'm gonna tell you what I told him. Not just anybody can do this. Although it's a great name and I'm probably gonna steal it.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he won't work with amateurs
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by stealing the amateur's idea
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy - rejecting while stealing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis Feinstein
dialogue.line: I don't work with amateurs. It's beneath me. I only collaborate with professionals who understand the candy business at my level.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis Feinstein
dialogue.line: Wait, what was that idea you just said? That's genius. I'm stealing that.

jokes.text: It smells like somebody spilled Chinese food in a bird cage.
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and disgusting comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and gross imagery for bad cologne

jokes.text: Portobello mushrooms. Where's the steak? Oh, there's no steak. That's a healthier option.
jokes.punchline: Chris's health-focused food betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Ron's meat obsession vs Chris's health obsession

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I was promised a steak dinner. A real steak. Thick cut. Aged properly.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Actually, I made a quinoa and kale salad with a tahini drizzle. It's locally sourced and has only 120 calories per serving.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Chris, I don't know what most of those words mean, and I'm concerned about the ones I do.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: It's really good for you! Studies show—

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I need to sit down. And drink a scotch. And reconsider our friendship.

jokes.text: My dad owns this place. I'm Janet. Janet Snakehole.
jokes.setup: April pretending to work at the club
jokes.punchline: Fake identity with ridiculous surname matching the venue
jokes.explanation: Classic April deception with perfectly absurd fake name

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Brandi Maxxxx, and I work here at the club.

jokes.text: We are like Robin Hood. We steal from the club and we give to ourselves.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining their free stuff scheme
jokes.punchline: Completely missing the point of Robin Hood's mission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic failure about heroic theft

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what? We're just like Robin Hood. We steal from the rich and give to the poor.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: That's not what we're doing.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, seriously. We're Robin Hood. I'm Robin Hood.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Robin Hood stole from corrupt tax collectors to return money to the people. You're taking free samples from Costco.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, but the principle is the same. I'm a hero.

jokes.text: I'm a human being. Sometimes I get blemishes. I'm not perfect.
jokes.setup: Ann finding concealer in Chris's medicine cabinet
jokes.punchline: Chris's defensive response about his imperfections
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Chris's vanity exposed through his defensive explanation.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris, why do you have concealer in your medicine cabinet?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I have pores, Ann. Large, cavernous pores. I'm not proud of it.

jokes.text: We broke up last week.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that destroys her entire perspective
jokes.explanation: Devastating simplicity that recontextualizes everything

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris and I have a really healthy relationship. We communicate, we respect each other, we're building something real.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ann, I'm moving to Washington, D.C. for a job at the National Park Service. I've already accepted.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: What? When were you going to tell me this?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I was going to tell you tonight. But also, I'm going to be seeing someone else there. We're not exclusive anymore.

jokes.text: Sweet and beautiful Ann has never been dumped before, and Chris is such a positive person, when he broke up with her, she just didn't realize it.
jokes.punchline: The intersection of Ann's inexperience and Chris's positivity creating miscommunication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character analysis that explains the absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: So Ann thinks Chris broke up with her, but Chris thinks they're still together because he was being positive about their relationship issues.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It's the perfect storm of Ann's relationship inexperience and Chris's relentless optimism.

jokes.text: although it does kind of make you wonder how good of a nurse she is
jokes.punchline: Questioning Ann's professional competence based on personal blindness
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that connects personal and professional incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ann is so oblivious to the world around her. Like, she didn't even notice that the entire office was decorated for her birthday party.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Which makes me question her medical competence. Like, how many times has she looked at someone and been like, 'Yeah, you're fine,' when they actually had like, a huge growth on their face?

jokes.text: A teriyaki hairpiece? I smell like the guy who's always coming up short.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of constant failure
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Tom self-awareness about his failures

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, my cologne smells like failure and broken dreams, which is basically my signature scent at this point. I've been wearing it since my first failed business venture, and I'm not about to change now. It's who I am.

jokes.text: I can smell your dreams, Tom. I can. And I can smell 'em from here. And honestly, they smell fucking terrible.
jokes.punchline: April's blunt destruction of his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - takes Tom's metaphor literally to devastate him

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I had big dreams, you know? I was going to be something special. I was going to soar like an eagle.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Eagles are just birds, Tom. They're not even that smart. I saw one fly into a window once.

jokes.text: One time, when I was in high school, a guy's mom called me and broke up with me for him.
jokes.punchline: Humiliating breakup via proxy parent
jokes.explanation: Perfectly humiliating breakup story that builds character sympathy

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: My mom called me to tell me that my boyfriend broke up with me. She found out from Facebook before I did.

jokes.text: Don't eat anything. Rebecca's coming. Who was Rebecca? Yeah. Exactly.
jokes.punchline: Mystery woman makes the rejection even more confusing and hurtful
jokes.explanation: Adds insult to injury with mysterious replacement

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: He broke up with me at a picnic. At a picnic!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's horrible. Who breaks up with someone at a picnic?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: A monster. But that's not even the worst part. He showed up with another woman.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? Who was she?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: I have no idea. He never introduced us. He just sat there eating potato salad with her while I stood there like an idiot.

jokes.text: Wait, wait. I worry what you just heard was, 'Give me a lot of bacon and eggs.' What I said was, give me all the bacon and eggs you have. Do you understand?
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that he literally means ALL the bacon and eggs
jokes.explanation: Iconic Ron Swanson moment - perfect escalation and character integration. Became a cultural reference.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Give me all the bacon and eggs you have.

dialogue.speaker: Server
dialogue.line: Do you want it on the side?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Bring me all the bacon and eggs you have. Wait, wait. I'm worried what you just heard was, bring me a lot of bacon and eggs. What I'm saying is, I want all the bacon and eggs you have. Do you understand?

jokes.text: He's gonna smell your dreams now.
jokes.setup: Tom about to spray Dennis with cologne
jokes.punchline: April referencing her earlier 'smelling dreams' insult
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that gives April's earlier insult new context

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Wait, Tom. I have a question: do you want to smell like dreams? Because that's what you're about to do to Dennis.

tmdb_overview: Ron and Leslie take a road trip to the big city in order to visit Ron's favorite place which happens to be a steakhouse, but Leslie has alternative motives for the trip.
insight: Indianapolis trip spawns 45 jokes in 45 minutes—character-driven absurdism at peak density.
